summary: Collapse the four read traversals into one read verb — temper explain <target>

edit.description: Add `Command::Explain { target }` — one read verb over the graph `check` computes (20-surface Decision: one read verb). Resolve one positional `<target>` across the four namespaces (member / requirement / leaf-grain address / neighborhood) by the ruled mechanism (John 2026-07-04, inbox): exactly one hit ⇒ explain it; multiple hits ⇒ ERROR listing each match's qualified spelling (`member:foo`, `requirement:foo`, …) for the retry; a qualified prefix is always accepted as an explicit spelling. Never guesses, never silently prefers a namespace. Projections, never gates; exits zero; discloses coverage (names what it cannot see).
Re-home the four traversals (why/requirements/impact/context) under `explain`, dispatched by the resolved target species. Reuse the graph-walk helpers left in place by CLI-COLLAPSE.

Reframe as explain tests: each target species walks its corner of the one graph; a member-vs-requirement name collision errors with both qualified spellings; a qualified prefix resolves directly; coverage disclosure asserted.

tests.asserts: explain <target> resolves member/requirement/address/neighborhood; a name collision errors listing qualified spellings; qualified prefixes accepted; never gates; discloses coverage

acceptance: `temper explain <target>` answers all four read questions over data check computes, exits zero, discloses coverage; an ambiguous bare target errors with each match's qualified spelling; a qualified prefix resolves directly; the four separate verbs are gone.
notes: Fork resolved (John 2026-07-04, inbox): bare target + collision error — cheap in the common ~95% case, explicit on ambiguity (git's ref-disambiguation ethos). Never guesses, never silently prefers. Terminal leaf; blocks nothing. An optional one-line corpus encode of the collision mechanism into the 20-surface 'one read verb' Decision is a human delta, not plan's to write.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm packages, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org + @temper scope, marketplace publish, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end; channel 1 (SDK publish) also rides the SDK-primary foundation

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution, 'install is two placements').

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery'). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering it. Scoped `@temper` names per the resolved `(project-name)` fork. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the SDK authoring-face manifest — channel 1's `temper` core + `@temper/claude-code` provider module).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`temper` + `@temper/claude-code`, floors as exported clause arrays); ch2 = engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human creds.
